subject: Project Management
title: Dark Agile
description: A quick win: understand Dark Agile.

hook.text: Some companies use Agile as a tool for surveillance — tracking velocity to pressure teams.

buildup.text: Managers monitor sprint burndowns, story points, and cycle times to squeeze more output.

discovery.text: Teams respond by gaming metrics — inflating estimates so they always 'hit the target.'

twist.text: Ron Jeffries, an Agile Manifesto signatory, warned developers to 'abandon Agile' if abused this way.

climax.text: Dark Agile happens when organizations adopt practices but reject the values underneath.

punchline.text: Agile without trust is just faster micromanagement.

quiz.question: What is 'Dark Agile'?
options: Using Agile practices to surveil and pressure teams
